_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national cron (Claude opus-4-7)
_meta.note: CA federal House batch — Lateefah Simon CA-12 freshman (Muslim, prog), Adam Gray CA-13 freshman Blue-Dog (HETERODOX Laken Riley YES + sole CA-Dem ending Nov 2025 shutdown + TPAction 2A 100% + CA Farm Bureau endorsed), Omed Hamid CA-11 Pelosi-seat LOST June 2 primary (iVoterGuide questionnaire hard evidence), Alberto Escobedo CA-13 NPP LOST June 2 primary (roster_verified — no policy record). Mike Thompson CA-4 SKIPPED 5th time (slug collision with mike-thompson MS-48 + KS-10 state senators — engine last-wins on slug would clobber wrong record; needs slug-disambig fix).

profile.religion: Muslim (4th Muslim member of U.S. Congress; first Muslim from California)
profile.background: Bay Area civil-rights advocate; legally blind from birth (first member of Congress with congenital legal blindness); MacArthur 'Genius' Fellow (2003, youngest woman recipient); JFK Frontier Leadership Award; California Woman of the Year. Youngest ED of Young Women's Freedom Center (age 19); ran SF DA Kamala Harris 'Back on Track' reentry program; ED of Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights of SF Bay Area; President of Akonadi Foundation and Meadow Fund. CSU Board of Trustees (2016, appointed Gov. Brown); BART Board 2016-2024 (President 2020-2024, launched Ambassador Program). BA Public Policy, Mills College; MPA, University of San Francisco. Single mother of two daughters. Vice Chair of the Congressional Progressive Caucus; Vice Chair of the Congressional Equality Caucus; Deputy Whip for Policy; member CBC, CAPAC, Democratic Women's Caucus. Sworn in January 2025 to succeed Barbara Lee in CA-12 (Oakland / East Bay).

lateefah-simon.notes_set: Heritage Action 119th Congress: 0% session / 0% lifetime score (party-line progressive). HARD-FALSE evidence on sanctity_of_life (cosponsor of H.R. 12 Women's Health Protection Act 2025; Planned Parenthood Action Fund + EMILY's List endorsed 2024), biblical_marriage (Vice Chair Equality Caucus; Rise Up for LGBTQI+ Youth in K-12 Schools resolution cosponsor), election_integrity Q1 (voted NO on H.R. 22 SAVE Act citizenship-proof voter registration), border_immigration (voted NO on FY26 DHS appropriations citing ICE/CBP overreach; explicit sanctuary support), self_defense (Giffords 'Champion Award' recipient; supports federal red-flag/universal background checks/safe storage). CROSS-DIRECTION (rare alignment with Christian-conservative restraint axis) on foreign_policy_restraint Q2/Q3: backs Block the Bombs Act to halt arms transfers to Israel; IMEU Policy Project 'top scores' on Palestinian rights; Emgage PAC endorsed (Muslim political org); on-record critic of AIPAC. christian_liberty cells held NULL (no auto-FALSE) — Muslim incumbent's silence on Christian-public-profession cells is not scoreable as opposition. Confirmed seeking reelection 2026; won June 2, 2026 CA-12 primary.

1.note: Cosponsor of H.R. 12 Women's Health Protection Act 2025 (codifies pre-viability abortion right); 'Reproductive Justice' House issue page vows to 'fight like hell' to restore Roe — opposite of abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund 2024 + EMILY's List endorsed/profiled — accepted abortion-industry institutional backing.

0.note: Vice Chair Congressional Equality Caucus; House issue page frames marriage equality as a core commitment.

1.note: Vice Chair Equality Caucus — institutional commitment to same-sex marriage.

2.note: 'Worked with Democratic colleagues to defeat anti-trans legislation'; cosponsor of Rise Up for LGBTQI+ Youth resolution.

4.note: Rise Up for LGBTQI+ Youth in K-12 Schools Initiative cosponsor — explicit support for LGBTQI+ identity promotion in schools.

2.note: Cosponsor of Rise Up for LGBTQI+ Youth in K-12 Schools resolution — explicitly promotes SOGI-aligned curriculum and policies.

1.note: Voted NO on H.R. 22 SAVE Act requiring documentary proof of citizenship for voter registration — opposite of photo-ID-plus-citizenship-verification position.

3.note: Same press release explicitly defends vote-by-mail and online registration — opposite of opposing mass mail-in voting.

0.note: Voted NO on FY26 DHS appropriations citing ICE/CBP funding as objectionable — opposite of supporting border enforcement build-up.

2.note: Explicit support for Alameda County / East Bay sanctuary stance against ICE cooperation — opposite of opposing sanctuary cities.

0.note: Giffords 'Champion Award' recipient; platform endorses universal background checks, safe-storage requirements, and federal red-flag (extreme risk) law — opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: Giffords-endorsed platform explicitly supports federal red-flag laws and 'closing loopholes' (registry/background-check expansion).

2.note: Pro-Giffords platform expands rather than repeals federal gun law (NFA/GCA preservation implicit).

Backs Block the Bombs Act to halt U.S. arms transfers to Israel — aligns with the restraint position opposing aid to foreign powers regardless of party convenience. CROSS-DIRECTION catch (a progressive Dem hitting the restraint cell from the anti-arms-export side).

3.note: No AIPAC endorsement; IMEU Policy Project rated her among top scores on Palestinian rights; Emgage PAC (Muslim political organization) endorsed — consistent with non-AIPAC funding pattern. CROSS-DIRECTION.

profile.background: Born September 23, 1977, Merced, California. Central Valley businessman; per TPAction listing, 'businessman and pistachio farmer.' Grew up in family dairy supply / feed store in Central Valley. BA Political Science, UC Santa Barbara; Merced College; Golden Valley HS (inaugural class). California State Assembly District 21 (Merced + parts of Stanislaus) 2012-2022 (term-limited); founded Sacramento bipartisan Problem Solvers Caucus; removed from Government Organization Committee chairmanship in 2020 for opposing Democratic water-policy cuts to San Joaquin Valley irrigation. Previously married to Cadee Condit, daughter of former Rep. Gary Condit; one child. Defeated incumbent John Duarte (R) by 187 votes in November 2024 — last U.S. House race called nationally (Dec 4, 2024). Sworn into 119th Congress January 2025. Blue Dog Coalition Whip; New Democrat Coalition; Future Forum. House Agriculture Committee (Vice Ranking Member, Livestock / Dairy / Poultry Subcommittee); House Natural Resources (Water / Wildlife / Fisheries).

adam-gray.notes_set: Among the most heterodox Democrats in the 119th Congress — score pattern stacks: Heritage Action 0% lifetime (party-line on Heritage's specific bill mix) yet TPAction overall 50% (2026 Live 55.56%), 2A 100%, Border Security 83.33%, Big Tech & Free Speech 100%. HARD cross-party TRUE catches: voted YES on Laken Riley Act (S.5 Roll Call 23, Jan 22 2025) — one of only 46 House Dems; ONLY California Democrat (and one of only 6 House Dems total) to vote YES on November 2025 shutdown-ending bill (222-209); California Farm Bureau endorsement 2024 + 2026 (very rare for any Democrat). Assembly-era: NRA 'B' average final cycles; Gun Owners of California 'stellar grades' 2016/2018/2020 (cited as 2014 GOC 'win'). HARD-FALSE catches: California Pro-Life Council 0% rating + Planned Parenthood Affiliates 93% rating (Assembly); Planned Parenthood Action Fund + Reproductive Freedom for All + Equality California endorsements 2024; CFT 93% / CTA 90% (teacher-union capture); voted NO on PROTECT Kids Act (gender-marker school notification); voted NO on FY26 budget resolution. Signature legislation: Valley Water Protection Act; HEATS Act (bipartisan geothermal w/ Young Kim R); bipartisan fentanyl crackdown bill (w/ Rep. Evans). Confirmed running 2026; won June 2 primary in CA-13 (will face Kevin Lincoln II R in November; Duarte is not running again).

1.note: Assembly-era California Pro-Life Council 0% rating; Planned Parenthood Affiliates 93% rating — pattern of consistent pro-choice voting, opposite of abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund + Reproductive Freedom for All 2024 — accepted abortion-industry institutional backing.

0.note: Equality California endorsement 2024 — institutional commitment to marriage equality (LGBTQ+).

4.note: Per TPAction, voted NO on PROTECT Kids Act (school gender-marker notification) — diverged from TPAction conservative position; supports LGBTQ+ policy promotion in schools.

0.note: California Federation of Teachers 93% + California Teachers Association 90% (Assembly career) — teacher-union capture, anti-school-choice posture.

1.note: Voted NO on PROTECT Kids Act (parental notification on gender-marker changes in school records) — opposite of parental-notification position.

Voted YES on Laken Riley Act (S.5, Roll Call 23, Jan 22 2025) mandating detention of inadmissible noncitizens charged with theft/violent crime — one of only 46 House Democrats. HARD cross-party deviation catch.

TPAction Second Amendment subscore 100% in 119th Congress (voted with 2A side on gun-restriction bills); Assembly-era Gun Owners of California 'stellar' grades + NRA 'B' average — HARD cross-party deviation on opposing new restrictions (red-flag / mag limits / AWB / registries).

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-11 (2026 D Candidate · open seat · Pelosi seat · LOST June 2 primary)

profile.religion: Not publicly stated (born in Kabul, Afghanistan; no faith self-identification in available sources)
profile.background: Born in Kabul, Afghanistan; came to U.S. for higher education. Technology advocate; founder of crowdsourcing platform Kabulay. 10+ years in tech/policy. Father of five children born/raised in San Francisco. Campaign branding: 'The Giraffe Vision' / 'Wider vision. Calmer leadership.' iVoterGuide rated him 'Leans Liberal.' LOST June 2, 2026 CA-11 top-two primary for the open Pelosi seat — Connie Chan (SF Supervisor) and Scott Wiener (CA State Senator) advanced to November general.

omed-hamid.notes_set: STATUS FIX: LOST June 2, 2026 CA-11 top-two primary; Chan + Wiener advanced. Pre-primary iVoterGuide questionnaire provided HARD-FALSE evidence on sanctity_of_life (pro-choice — abortion 'should remain legal and protected'; opposed Comstock Act repeal), biblical_marriage Q0/Q1 ('strongly disagree' marriage is God-ordained one-man-one-woman; supports marriage equality for all consenting adults), Q2 (disagreed transgender participation in women's sports constitutes unfairness), border_immigration Q2 (backs sanctuary cities — 'help build trust between local communities and law enforcement'), self_defense Q0/Q1 (supports universal background checks, closing loopholes, federal red-flag, safe storage). No evidence on foreign policy / Israel / AIPAC (notable gap given Afghan-origin). No religious self-identification.

0.note: iVoterGuide questionnaire: abortion 'should remain a legal and protected healthcare option, particularly in the early stages' — opposite of conception personhood.

1.note: iVoterGuide: opposed the Comstock Act as 'an outdated 19th-century law' — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'strongly disagree' with statement that 'Marriage is a God-ordained...union of one man and one woman'; explicitly supports marriage equality for all consenting adults.

1.note: iVoterGuide: explicit support for marriage equality (same-sex marriage) for all consenting adults.

2.note: iVoterGuide: disagreed that transgender participation in women's sports constitutes unfairness — opposite of affirming biological sex as immutable.
iVoterGuide: 'Sanctuary policies help build trust between local communities and law enforcement' — opposite of opposing sanctuary cities.

0.note: iVoterGuide: supports 'universal background checks, closing loopholes...red flag laws...safe storage requirements' — opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: iVoterGuide: supports federal red-flag laws and 'closing loopholes' — directly opposite of opposing red-flag laws and registries.

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-13 (2026 No Party Pref Candidate · LOST June 2 primary · Gray + Lincoln advanced)

profile.background: Merced, California resident. NPP (No Party Preference) independent challenger to incumbent Adam Gray (D) and Kevin Lincoln II (R) in CA-13. Limited public policy record. Spoke publicly in apparent support of the Laken Riley Act (June 21, 2025 YouTube clip) — pro-enforcement border lean. LOST June 2, 2026 top-two primary; did not appear in the California Secretary of State results table. Gray (40.9%) and Lincoln (29.1%) advanced to the November general election.

alberto-escobedo.notes_set: STATUS FIX: LOST June 2, 2026 CA-13 top-two primary; Gray (D) + Lincoln (R) advanced. Almost no policy record located — no campaign website, social media accounts, candidate questionnaire (Vote411/LWV), or endorsement list surfaced in search. One ambiguous data point: June 21, 2025 YouTube clip titled 'Mr. Alberto Escobedo of Merced Spoke On the Laken Riley Act' — content suggests pro-enforcement border lean but transcript not extractable; flagged SOFT only and not scored. Record exits active queue via status: lost; confidence stays roster_verified honestly (real candidate, real status fix, but no policy positions to score).
